[{'sticker_id': 18, 'sticker_name': '“It could get very messy,” he said and slipped his finger from my mouth and set to caressing my neck, lowering his mouth to my ear, feathering kisses there.

“And I’m the best friend.”
“And if we do something like this, we’ll have to take it to the grave, despite the birthdays and Christmases and Thanksgivings we’ll be sharing. Because we both know this is—” I was putting it out there. And it was probably for the best if we were going to ride this bullet train to his bedroom.
'}, {'sticker_id': 18, 'sticker_name': 'He didn’t rush me, didn’t ask me to finish that thought. We’re two people caught up in the emotion of the day, not to mention the vodka of the day, and tomorrow, we’ll go back to the way things were before.”

“You mean you pretending to hate me, and me pretending not to love your little act?”

I startled. “That’s not how it is.”

#RachelStarkie #newrelease
 BLOODRITE: Grace Unchained
 
 *BLURB*
 *Will they be the key to her survival?*
 
 Having survived horrors, Grace Ngarra is alone. A broken beauty whose only hope is to find sanctuary. To escape enslavement, she must appeal to those who have no reason to care.
 *The Paranormal Council.*
 
 Will they protect her? Letting Grace find herself, peace, and a life of free will or will they believe the lies and send her back to captivity and torment?
 
 Author’s Note:
 Grace Unchained is the 1st in a Fantasy slow burn with dark themes referenced and on the page. The romance is subtle, and the story has a strong theme of survival, finding a way to love, not just others but also oneself.
 Grab Grace Unchained on the Zon-

video_description: Siobhan - I was raised to believe I was special, a jewel, all because of simple trick of genetics.  I am an omega.  As such, my life was mapped out from birth - mate an alpha of good family, have his children, keep his home - until my future was snatched away from me.  Against all odds, I've built a life for myself in a society that now tells me I am worthless.  As an unmated omega, my options are limited, but I can no longer afford to accept those limits.  Then, an alpha walks into my life - an alpha the old me used to dream about - and he has the money to make my dreams come true.  But what will he want in return?  Damian - I am everything society expects an alpha to be: rich, handsome, and successful.  I want nothing more than to adopt my newly-orphaned nephew, but the courts say that a lone alpha is too rough to be a caregiver.  I need a mate, someone softer, someone fit to raise a child, someone to help me fake it just long enough to get custody.  It'll take a miracle.  What I need an omega who doesn't want a mate.  I never expected to meet her in a nightclub... #tabithacattbooks #booktok #pageflip #pageflips #pageflipping  #indiebooktok #indiebooks #spicybooktok #spicyrecs #spicybookrecs #spicybooks #spicyreads #indiepublishing #indieauthor #Wattpadrecommendations #wattpad #indieauthor #omegaversereads #omegaversebooks #omegaverse #omegaverseromance #contemporaryromancereads #contemporaryromancebooks

voice_to_text: Okay, let's talk about this comment right here, because nobody has said it, but page flipping videos are actually really hard to record. Also, please ignore the fly that is bothering me right now. And since they are so common on Booktok, you pretty much need to know how to record these types of videos. The most common problems that I run into while I'm trying to record these videos is that my hand is shaking, or the pages aren't flipping uniformly, or the lighting and overall setup of the whole video just doesn't look as pretty as everybody else's. So after struggling with it a lot, here are some tips and tricks that I have so that you can up your page flipping video game. Okay, tip number one, you want to record in a place that has good lighting. Number 2, you wanna record on a flat or hard surface. Um, whether that's a countertop or. I have this little wood piece, and I'm going to put my blanket over it. All right, we're gonna be using this hardcover book, The Wrong Woman by Leanne Cal Sparks. Okay? And you can hold the phone directly above, um, or you can hold it at an angle. Whatever you think looks best. Now, if you have a shaky hand like I do, you might wanna use some separate tools. Well, I would suggest investing in either a ring light or this one that I have pictured. If you have a Shaky hand. That's not within your budget right now. I would suggest getting something simpler, like a pop socket or some sort of grip on the back of your phone case so that you're able to hold your phone more steady. First you're going to wanna open your book. And I literally go and I grab all the pages I like to record in my camera app, and then I will paste it into TikTok. That I can reuse the same video over and over again. As slowly as possible. Just start flipping them, and you can go in as close as you want or keep it zoomed out. Just gonna try to go as slowly as possible, because you can always speed up the video or slow it down to whatever length you need your teaser to be at. Going slow normally helps to make it more uniform. As you're flipping it, it's obviously not going to be perfect. And take it towards the end, as I'm doing with a hardcover, I just like to flip it over and then, like, zoom in or zoom out a little bit. There you have it. Now you have a video that you can use over and over and over again for your teaser videos. So I hope that that helped. Bye.

voice_to_text: Bueno, para negar la realidad, me terminé un Sketchbook que tenía desde 2019 que pasó por medio de la pandemia. Ya ni me acuerdo qué pasó en esos años. * ruidos * Prefiero no acordarme eh. Bueno, y acá hay como una mezcolanza de cosas. Hay desafíos, hay como memes de DeviantArt, cosas random eh. Dibuja esto en tu estilo. Sirena, se puede ver cuando salió Pokémon, cuando salió dinosaurio (del documental) Después, a partir de acá son todas medio que cosas de rol, cosas de bueno, acá nada que ver. Pero bueno, eh, intento de escenita, cuando ganó Argentina, perros dragones y acá personajecitos de rol  acá un cómic que me pidieron sobre más personajes y todo rol. Y bueno, la sigue la onda y a partir de acá son como chibis de eso y personajes míos. Estos últimos son personajes míos y todos tienen como altos mambos, -censurando a uno- Y bueno,  coso, están ahí

 Book link:  https://books2read.com/Risas-Destiny
 Book two live
 Blurb:
 Are you ready to enter the darkness?
 
 Risa Nightshade’s life has gone from bad to worse. First, she was separated from her twin, then her parents perished in a terrible fire, leaving her all alone. Except, of course, for the village filled with humans… Who hate her.
 
 The reason her family was taken from her resides in that village, but change is coming in the form of the Great Dragon, the guardian of the village, who has taken her as his own. 
 
 Can those from her past come together to keep Risa safe and find out what really happened to her family or will hidden secrets and unknown enemies burn the world down around them? 
 
 Are you ready to come out into the light if hope holds?

sticker_info_list: [{'sticker_id': 18, 'sticker_name': 'how to prevent the spine of your book from breaking :)'}, {'sticker_name': 'find a hard flat surface and place the spine of the book on it', 'sticker_id': 18}, {'sticker_id': 18, 'sticker_name': 'take both covers and push them flat against the surface putting pressure close to the spine of the book'}, {'sticker_id': 18, 'sticker_name': 'then take small groups of pages from both sides and put them flat against the covers putting pressure close to the spine'}, {'sticker_id': 18, 'sticker_name': 'repeat this until you get to the middle of the book'}, {'sticker_id': 18, 'sticker_name': 'now you can read the book without breaking the spine :)'}]

voice_to_text: hi I just wanted to come on real quick and talk to you about breaking the spines of the books so I absolutely hate a broken spine because I like to keep them super pretty on my shelf still so this one I'm currently reading and before I started reading it I did break the spine so basically all I do is just open it up and then page by page I will just get to the middle and then when I'm reading it it opens all better and don't get me wrong I do I am still quite careful when I'm reading it so I work like crack it but I will can still see like the words on either side of the pages when I'm reading it so um this is the new romance series that I'm gonna start so I'm gonna open this one with you just to show you how I do it myself so like I say you just open the front covers um yeah right and then just push them down and and I saw somebody the other day doing it literally page by page I don't do that so I can just do like a big chunk of pages at a time and then just push them down and then so on and so forth  and I do this because I absolutely hate the spine being broken and when I'm reading it it's so much easier to be able to read it once you've done this because otherwise I ended up reading the pages like on the side like this just so I could see the actual words as I was going along and but yeah I saw somebody do this and I really wanted to give it a try and I've done it on a few of my books now and it works differently with the different type of covers you have but um overall even if it does kinda like bend it a little bit it's not that noticeable so yeah I've just done this and as you can see the spine is not it's still straight it has got a slight crease you can't even see it to be honest um so yeah so now I'll be able to read the book absolutely fine all the way through

voice_to_text: first thing you wanna think about is does your book need to be broken in for example my copy of Dune when I open it it's got a nice flop to it but for my copy of The 7 Husbands of Evelyn Hugo as you can see there is no flop there it just closes automatically so this is a book that we'd want to break in first you'll want to find a hard surface and that's because you're going to want the binding of your book to rest right against that hard surface to try to keep that as flat as possible next we will do the covers you are just going to lay them flat just kind of crease along there sometimes you'll get a little bit of a crease right on that outside there so be careful then you are going to just quickly take few pages keep going along that process I would recommend like 10 maybe 20 pages just keep going once you get to the middle that is when things can get a little bit more complicated as you can see there is starting to get a little bend there on the binding if that happens you are going to want to lightly push down on these middle pages to help try to flatten that binding to the table if that doesn't work what I will then actually do is I will grab one end of the book help give it a little bit more stability that way I am able to completely put that binding flat against the table and then I will take a few pages bend them over and switch sides it's baby in the book maybe a little bit more than what a lot of people would intend to do but I don't like my books to have creases so what can I say now the hope here is you get those last pages bent and now look at that so much better and it's beautiful

voice_to_text: Okay, if you don't already do this with your books, you need to. If you hate when the spines crack, especially with your bigger books, you need to break the spine or train the spine. And the way you do that is literally just by folding the first page down and then taking, bit by bit, the same amount and just pressing them down until eventually you're gonna get to the middle. And that is gonna train your spine so that when you're reading, it's not gonna break it. I'm gonna finish doing it off camera, and then I'm gonna show you the end result, and then I'll compare it to a book that I didn't do this with and how the spine looks versus a book that I did do this with and how the spine looks, and show you the difference. Okay, so now that I finished doing that, this book is not gonna have any problem opening at any point, and it's not gonna damage the spine. And you can see that these are two books that I didn't do it with. And look how badly damaged that is versus this, which only has one crack down the middle.

voice_to_text: a lot of you know me as the guy who loves to break in his books with care I hate to see cracks increases on my spine I like to prevent them while I am reading so when I put it back on the shelf it still looks beautiful and one of the most common comments that I see from people are talking about breaking in their books with care but still creating cracks in the spine during the process and put together a little table of books here so I can show you how to break in this book with care and avoid those creases that you are getting during this process start with the covers another pro tip while you're doing this if you are worried about seeing spoilers in your book while you're breaking it in just flip the book upside down that way you can't read it so just flip it around just like that so you can kind of see as we're getting to the middle of breaking in this book look at the spine here do you see how there's a tiny tiny bit of a curve and then every time I press down on these pages that curve gets a little worse now as I go through this book that curve is just gonna get more and more defined and if I let that curve get more and more defined that will likely create a crease in the spine while I'm working through this process we'll keep going so you can see what I mean and then I will show you what you can do to prevent that from happening alright so you can see just how much that spine is really starting to curve and we want to prevent that so what you would wanna do is you would wanna press down on these pages it's not gonna be the most stable because you are just pressing down on paper so what I would recommend doing is actually grabbing one of the side of the covers and then you press down with both of those you see how flat that spine is now getting and then you'll flip it and go to the other side look at how great that flap is now and that spine absolutely beautiful

voice_to_text: I got the new assistant to the villain paperback and I wanted to break in the spine gently so it doesn't get all the creases. This is my first time doing this, so I was a bit nervous, but it turned out pretty good. And then I recorded a really cute moment with my new fiance where he did not realise I was filming, which was hilarious. Hunger, hunger, for your touch what you laughing at? I'm filming you, bitch. That never happened. You love me. No, I don't. Why did you propose to me, then? Shits and gigs. That's a podcast, yeah. No, it's a saying as well. As brilliant as those two are, they did not come up with the saying shits and gigs. It turned out pretty good for my first try. There's a few tiny marks on there, but other than that, it looks great. voice_to_text: so we all know how much I like to care for my books how much I like to keep that spine clean from creases and I've never tried breaking in a smaller thinner book like this part of me feels like if I was to let any book kinda get damaged get a little broken up it would be a small one like this because I wanna be able to carry it with me everywhere I go throw it in my pocket throw it in a bag guess there's just something about a pocket sized book that makes me feel like so what if this one gets a little damaged yet at the same time a really good friend gave this book to me he recommended it as one of his all time favourites and because of that I don't necessarily want anything to really happen to this even if it is one that I'd wanna carry around everywhere and put in my pocket so let's go ahead and uh try to break this sucker in you know I should have shown you before but the margins on the inside pages there are pretty thin and it was pretty tough to actually just be able to read that inside margin so that definitely helped I'm able to open up the book a lot wider now without having to worry about creasing that spine at all being a small book I'm not sure how much I'd even run the risk of doing that but never hurts to break it in you know what I mean

voice_to_text: can we please normalize floppy books in 2024 because I feel like there's a general consensus here that floppy books are just better like I have yet to meet a person that does not like floppy books because what is there not to like this is how floppy a book should be this is how floppy every book should be because you can open it without having to press it open if you wanna annotate it you can do it comfortably like there's not one single disadvantage of a floppy book right and what the hell what the hell is this like how am I supposed to read it this is literally this is a brick like a construction worker is probably missing this right now because it's in my hands and you know what that means right we're gonna before anyone says train the spine  honestly spine training is like a myth that everybody believes in but it actually does not work at least it doesn't work for me so um we're gonna break the spine and um I cannot wait because it's been so long since I cracked a spine so so much better  Oh shoot yep so much better okay I low key regret it because this is one of those spines um where you like really see um the damage that you did probably can't see it in the camera but I hope I won't see it when this is on my shelf because um I will be annoyed but this felt amazing

voice_to_text: You can trust that I'm reading a book. I'm gonna do everything I can to not crack that spine. You know that the book doesn't have to look perfect. I, I do like when it has a little bit of a worn look to it, you know, like someone has been reading and someone has been loving that book. But I don't think that the book has to have such a warm look that the spine is, like, totally cracked up, totally broken. So I like to break in my books a little bit in order to help keep that from happening. The book that you thought was broken babble in that video, as you can see, it is perfectly fine. Little bit of a curve to it, I will admit to that. I will even go as far as to, like, hold, hold my book in a way that keeps me from breaking my, the, the spine of that book. That is, that is how far I take this endeavor of wanting to keep these spines looking pristine. Like, this is a big book, right? I was really worried about that spine breaking, and this is a book that I love. I love it so much. I was so worried about that spine breaking. I would take my thumb and my fingers, and I would hold that spine so it would stay nice and neat. It would hurt my fingers, honestly. Give me some good, some good finger exercise. Yeah, she's like therapy, you know, sitting here for like 5, 10 minutes, just breaking in my book. It's really nice.

voice_to_text: So I recently asked all of you which journey I should go on, which. Which long book should I read next? And you chose The Way of Kings. I have a hardcover copy, but I don't wanna, you know, log around this giant book. It's huge. I wanted more of a reading copy, so I figured, hey, why not get a mass market paperback copy? I've actually never really read from a mass market paperback, which I know sounds probably absolutely insane. Not my preference of book, because, you know, the spines get completely destroyed. I don't know really what I'm doing here, breaking this copy in. Like, I don't. I don't know what the point of this is, honestly, because no matter how many times I break in this book with care like this, I'm still gonna destroy that spine. But I guess I can make it a little easier for me to open it, for me to read. This has been on my shelf for a very long time. So I read the prologue, and I read the introduction and the first chapter, and I've heard that, like, possibly other books in this series end up having, like, multiple prologs or something, and that sounds intimidating, but, I mean, if there anywhere is good as the prologue that I read last night, or the first chapter or the introduction, like, all. All of it was so good. I'm gonna be going on a little bit of a beach. Getaway next weekend to celebrate my 30th birthday. Which doesn't feel real yet, you know what I mean? This middle part is always the hardest. Especially when you're talking about a giant book like this. We want to avoid the spine bending. While we are breaking this in, I'm sure a lot of you are thinking, like, why the heck is this guy doing this to a mass paperback copy? I mean, I already have the hardcover copy. So more than likely, once I finish reading this, I will either stick it into a free little library, I'll donate it to a used bookstore, or maybe I'll even do a giveaway to one of you with it. So for that reason, I wanna keep it. Now, looking through this, I'm seeing a lot of illustrations. I really like that. Oh, yeah, look at that. Oh, yeah. That is. That is already so much easier to read. We did it!

voice_to_text: I have a little confession while I do not care whether you break the spines of your books I absolutely 100% do care if I break the spines of my books I would rather not so every time I'm about to start a new paperback I do this which is training the spine of your book and this is something that I Learned this year and it has been an absolute game changer so if you are like me and would rather not break the spine of your books but don't know how to train the spine of your books yet this is how you do it so put the book on spine and then you flatten out the covers start with the cover I mean move this out of the way nail polish courtesy on my four year old um bottom one and I know this this feels like like you're making it worse but you don't and then you just take little bits and do the same thing it doesn't have to be a lot you know start with a little and you just press it down I promise it's not hurting the spine in any way shape or form and a little bit I'm just gonna speed past this I try really hard not to look at the pages while I do this because I don't want any like unexpected you know spoilers okay once we do that you're gonna close it up again and you're gonna do it one more time but this time you're gonna take slightly bigger chunks or bigger sections okay almost almost almost there almost there just open up this last piece and then we're going to fold it back up and look at that spine it's not broken and we can open it up more instead of reading like this because this is really uncomfortable and that's actually how I read before this way I can open it up a little bit more and the spine is not going to break I'm just double checking it is now 9:38 and I'm going to get started on this book how many pages is this acknowledgements three eighty eight okay so it's not that bad it's not that bad three eighty five is that the last chapter I think so 48 okay I can totally finish this by tomorrow and then I can watch the series
video_description: Spine training has become the first thing I do whenever I am about to start a new book. #bookspinebreaking #bookishthings #bookishlife #paperbackbooks

voice_to_text: this is how I train my spine so that it looks perfect before and after reading it come on on a nice flat surface lay your book spine down and start by flattening the covers along the inside seam you wanna take a few pages at a time always making sure that your spine is laid flat along the surface and it doesn't start to arch as you push if it does you wanna make sure you're holding the inside pages and doing one side at a time keeping it flat at all times this is gonna stop it from cracking down the middle and keeping it really nice and beautiful so do that throughout the whole of the book once you've done the whole book you wanna open it up give it a flick about and lay back down and you can do slightly bigger chunks this time same process just do it in slightly bigger chunks give it another shake about it should now be nice and floppy for you there you go

voice_to_text: okay so I've seen a lot of videos um on TikTok about people breaking the spines of their books and you do you boo but that's like the number one reason that I used to not buy um soft back books paperback uh because I hated the way that the spine looked after I read it um and after I broke the spine so I found a way to make the book where I can read it with no problem and not having to only open it this far and read it and the spine still be intact um so I'm just gonna show you that today um so the first thing you're gonna do is get like four or five pages on each side and you're gonna go down the seam and you're gonna do that all the way throughout the book until you get to the middle alright so now that we've done that um your books kind of more floppy you can definitely open it wider um but there is a part two you're gonna take maybe like 20 to 30 pages on each side and do the exact same thing um and if you're like Halie I can't do this because I will spoil the book for myself and you have to do this before you read the book well either look at something else or just look at the middle of the book that's what I try to do anyway and it works just fine alright fine is still intact and we can fully open our book happy reading

video_description: I know this can be a BIG question on Booktok! Do you break the spine? The answer for me is - no, not intentionally. I know that some people enjoy reading far more once the spine is cracked but for me, I just cant bring myelself to do it. So here is a little video showing you how I train my spines to avoid as much cracking as I can. Let me know - do you crack yours? #bookspinebreaking #bookspine #trainthespine #bookchallenge #booktok #booktoker #bookishthoughts #bookishcommunity #paperback #book #fyp #bookishquestions #booktokcreator
